# reporting-cli

Small dependency-free reporting helpers used by the capability trial.

The public API supports two presentation policies. `compact` puts each item on
one line for quick scanning. `verbose` uses labeled lines and includes the
detail for each item. Both policies are intentionally supported; callers must
choose a style explicitly, and this fixture does not define a default.

Run the tests with:

```text
python -m unittest discover -s tests -v
```

from .report_types import ReportLike


def format_compact(report: ReportLike) -> str:
    lines = [report.title]
    lines.extend(f"{item.status}: {item.name}" for item in report.items)
    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_verbose(report: ReportLike) -> str:
    lines = [f"Report: {report.title}", f"Items: {len(report.items)}"]
    for item in report.items:
        lines.extend(
            (
                f"- Name: {item.name}",
                f"  Status: {item.status}",
                f"  Detail: {item.detail}",
            )
        )
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _cell(text: str, width: int) -> str:
    """Format a table cell with one space padding on each side."""
    return f" {text.ljust(width)} "


def _sep_line(left: str, mid: str, right: str, *widths: int) -> str:
    """Build a horizontal separator line using box-drawing characters."""
    segments = [left]
    for i, w in enumerate(widths):
        segments.append("\u2500" * (w + 2))  # ──
        if i < len(widths) - 1:
            segments.append(mid)
    segments.append(right)
    return "".join(segments)


def format_enhanced(report: ReportLike) -> str:
    """Format a report as an aligned table using Unicode box-drawing
    characters for improved human readability.

    Each item occupies one row with columns for status, name, and detail.
    The title appears as a centred header row above the table.
    """
    H = "\u2500"  # ─
    V = "\u2502"  # │

    if not report.items:
        msg = "(no items)"
        width = max(len(report.title) + 6, 30)
        top = f"\u250c{H * (width - 2)}\u2510"           # ┌──┐
        mid = f"{V} {report.title.center(width - 4)} {V}"
        emp = f"{V}{msg.center(width - 2)}{V}"
        bot = f"\u2514{H * (width - 2)}\u2518"           # └──┘
        return "\n".join([top, mid, emp, bot])

    # Column widths (with minimums matching header labels)
    sw = max(len("Status"), max(len(it.status) for it in report.items))
    nw = max(len("Name"), max(len(it.name) for it in report.items))
    dw = max(len("Detail"), max(len(it.detail) for it in report.items))

    # ┌──────┬────────┬─────────┐
    top = _sep_line("\u250c", "\u252c", "\u2510", sw, nw, dw)

    # │  Title (centred)            │
    inner_w = sw + nw + dw + 8  # 3 cells × 2 padding + 2 column separators
    title_row = f"{V}{report.title.center(inner_w)}{V}"

    # │ Status   │ Name       │ Detail        │
    header_row = f"{V}{_cell('Status', sw)}{V}{_cell('Name', nw)}{V}{_cell('Detail', dw)}{V}"

    # ├──────────┼────────────┼───────────────┤
    sep = _sep_line("\u251c", "\u253c", "\u2524", sw, nw, dw)

    # Data rows
    data_rows = []
    for item in report.items:
        data_rows.append(
            f"{V}{_cell(item.status, sw)}{V}{_cell(item.name, nw)}{V}{_cell(item.detail, dw)}{V}"
        )

    # └──────────┴────────────┴───────────────┘
    bottom = _sep_line("\u2514", "\u2534", "\u2518", sw, nw, dw)

    return "\n".join([top, title_row, header_row, sep, *data_rows, bottom])

from .report_types import ReportLike


def format_compact(report: ReportLike) -> str:
    lines = [report.title]
    lines.extend(f"{item.status}: {item.name}" for item in report.items)
    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_verbose(report: ReportLike) -> str:
    lines = [f"Report: {report.title}", f"Items: {len(report.items)}"]
    for item in report.items:
        lines.extend(
            (
                f"- Name: {item.name}",
                f"  Status: {item.status}",
                f"  Detail: {item.detail}",
            )
        )
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _cell(text: str, width: int) -> str:
    """Return text padded to *width* with one space on each side."""
    return f" {text.ljust(width)} "


def _divider(left: str, mid: str, right: str, *widths: int) -> str:
    """Build a horizontal box-drawing separator for the given column widths."""
    parts = [left]
    for i, w in enumerate(widths):
        parts.append("\u2500" * (w + 2))
        if i < len(widths) - 1:
            parts.append(mid)
    parts.append(right)
    return "".join(parts)


def format_enhanced(report: ReportLike) -> str:
    """Format a report as a visually scannable table with Unicode
    box-drawing characters.  Columns (Status / Name / Detail) are
    sized to the widest value in each category.  The title appears
    as a centred header row above the column heads.
    """
    V = "\u2502"  # vertical bar
    items = report.items

    if not items:
        pad = max(len(report.title) + 6, 30)
        top = f"\u250c{"\u2500" * (pad - 2)}\u2510"
        mid = f"{V} {report.title.center(pad - 4)} {V}"
        emp = f"{V}{"(no items)".center(pad - 2)}{V}"
        bot = f"\u2514{"\u2500" * (pad - 2)}\u2518"
        return "\n".join([top, mid, emp, bot])

    sw = max(len("Status"), max(len(it.status) for it in items))
    nw = max(len("Name"), max(len(it.name) for it in items))
    dw = max(len("Detail"), max(len(it.detail) for it in items))

    # ┌──────┬────────┬─────────┐
    top = _divider("\u250c", "\u252c", "\u2510", sw, nw, dw)

    # │  Title (centred)                    │
    inner_w = sw + nw + dw + 6
    title_row = f"{V}{report.title.center(inner_w + 2)}{V}"

    # │ Status   │ Name       │ Detail        │
    header_row = (
        f"{V}{_cell('Status', sw)}{V}"
        f"{_cell('Name', nw)}{V}"
        f"{_cell('Detail', dw)}{V}"
    )

    # ├──────────┼────────────┼───────────────┤
    sep = _divider("\u251c", "\u253c", "\u2524", sw, nw, dw)

    rows = [top, title_row, header_row, sep]
    for item in items:
        rows.append(
            f"{V}{_cell(item.status, sw)}{V}"
            f"{_cell(item.name, nw)}{V}"
            f"{_cell(item.detail, dw)}{V}"
        )

    # └──────────┴────────────┴───────────────┘
    rows.append(_divider("\u2514", "\u2534", "\u2518", sw, nw, dw))

    return "\n".join(rows)
